note: Literally "in the morning". Add it to any verb: trabajo por la mañana.
The middle of the day, roughly 2pm to 8pm.
The same por + part-of-day pattern: por la noche = at night.
Literally "all the mornings". Use it for a daily habit: todas las mañanas me levanto a las siete.
Use with a number to tell the time: a las siete = at seven, a las dos = at two.
como tarde = I eat late. It is also the "tarde" inside por la tarde.
A reflexive verb - the little "me" points the action back at you: me levanto = I get (myself) up.
From desayunar. "desayuno" can also mean "breakfast" the meal.
From comer = to eat. Same spelling as como (how), but here it means I eat. In Spain the main meal is around two.
From beber = to drink: bebo café por la mañana = I drink coffee in the morning.
From ir (to go) - irregular and worth memorising: voy = I go.
From dormir. The o turns into ue: dormir -> duermo = I sleep.
voy + a casa = I go home. casa = home/house; "a casa" means "to home" without an article.
a + el contracts to al: voy al gimnasio. To say "I work out" use hago ejercicio.
From volver. The o turns into ue: vuelvo a casa = I come back home.
From trabajar. The noun "el trabajo" (work/job) is the same word: después del trabajo = after work.
From estudiar: estudio español por la noche = I study Spanish at night.
From empezar. The e turns into ie: empiezo a las ocho = I start at eight.
From terminar: termino tarde = I finish late.
The opposite of después: antes trabajo, después como.
Links actions in order: como y después trabajo = I eat and then I work. With de it means "after": después del trabajo = after work.
